title: Orange Pi full sunxi-mmc driver audit

findings.finding: The Orange Pi full tree's sunxi-mmc driver does not add A733-specific normal-IDMAC logic. It contains the ordinary mainline-style descriptor setup and DMA start sequence.
findings.decision: No A733 behavior patch is justified from this tree.

findings.finding: Compared against the local mainline baseline, the visible diff is mostly host allocation/removal/runtime-PM API churn. It does not name a new register, reset, fabric bit, or descriptor format requirement.
findings.decision: Do not port lifecycle/API churn into the diagnostic branch.

findings.finding: The Orange Pi full tree zeros the last descriptor next pointer. Prior local evidence already covered practical vendor/mainline descriptor shapes, including single-descriptor and chain cases with terminal next=0 in the chain dump. The controller still failed before descriptor consumption.
findings.decision: Do not retest last-descriptor next=0 without new evidence.

conclusion: No kernel patch was made. The Orange Pi full driver audit adds no source-backed IDMAC descriptor-fetch delta.
